name: incident-response-incident-response
description: Use when working with incident response incident response
capabilities: Coordinate multi-agent incident response across detection, investigation, resolution, and communication phases
Classify incident severity (P0-P3) and determine appropriate response protocols
Orchestrate observability analysis using distributed tracing, metrics, and log aggregation
Guide root cause analysis using Five Whys methodology and system debugging
Generate blameless postmortem documentation with action items and process improvements
Implement monitoring enhancements and system hardening to prevent incident recurrence
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 11 static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate incident response workflow guide (markdown documentation). The 'external_commands' detection refers to markdown backticks for file paths, not shell execution. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'system/network reconnaissance' detections are scanner misinterpretations of incident response terminology (severity levels, observability analysis, root cause analysis). No actual security risks present.
valueStatement: This skill provides a structured multi-phase incident response workflow for AI agents, enabling rapid detection, investigation, resolution, and postmortem documentation following modern SRE principles.
limitations: This is a workflow guide only - it does not execute actual system operations
Requires other specialized skills for specific tasks (deployment, security scanning, debugging)
Does not have direct access to monitoring systems or incident management tools
Relies on Claude/Codex agent orchestration, not direct infrastructure access

useCases.title: SRE Team Lead managing production outage
useCases.description: Use the full workflow to coordinate team response, maintain incident command structure, and ensure proper communication during a sev-1 incident.

useCases.title: DevOps Engineer conducting post-incident review
useCases.description: Use Phase 5 (Postmortem & Prevention) to document incident timeline, identify root causes, and create action items for monitoring improvements.

useCases.title: On-call engineer performing initial triage
useCases.description: Use Phase 1 (Detection & Triage) to quickly classify incident severity, assess impact, and determine initial mitigation steps.

promptTemplates.title: Initial Incident Triage
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the incident-response skill to triage this alert: [DESCRIBE ALERT]. Determine severity level (P0-P3), identify affected services, assess user impact, and recommend initial mitigation actions.
promptTemplates.scenario: When receiving an alert from PagerDuty, Opsgenie, or monitoring system

promptTemplates.title: Deep Investigation Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the incident-response skill to investigate this incident: [INCIDENT DESCRIPTION]. Conduct deep debugging, security assessment, and performance analysis to identify root cause.
promptTemplates.scenario: When initial triage is complete and detailed investigation is needed

promptTemplates.title: Emergency Deployment Coordination
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the incident-response skill to coordinate this emergency fix: [INCIDENT AND FIX DESCRIPTION]. Execute deployment with validation, monitoring, and rollback readiness.
promptTemplates.scenario: When a fix is ready to be deployed to production

promptTemplates.title: Postmortem Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the incident-response skill to conduct a blameless postmortem for: [INCIDENT SUMMARY]. Document timeline, root cause, what went well, what could improve, and create action items.

bestPractices: Assign clear incident commander and roles within the first 5 minutes of any P0/P1 incident
Update stakeholder communication every 15-30 minutes during active incidents
Complete blameless postmortem within 48 hours with specific, assignable action items
antiPatterns: Skipping severity classification and jumping straight to debugging without understanding impact
Blaming individuals in postmortems rather than focusing on system improvements
Delaying communication to stakeholders until full resolution is achieved

faq.question: Does this skill execute actual incident response actions?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides workflow guidance and orchestrates other specialized skills. It does not directly access monitoring systems, execute deployments, or modify infrastructure.

faq.question: Can this skill replace my incident management platform?
faq.answer: No. This skill is designed to work with existing tools like PagerDuty, Opsgenie, and status pages. It provides the workflow logic but relies on external systems for alerting and communication.

faq.question: What severity levels does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports P0 (SEV-1) through P3 (SEV-4) severity levels. P0/P1 require immediate all-hands response, while P2/P3 follow standard response procedures.

faq.question: How does this skill handle security incidents?
faq.answer: Phase 2 includes a Security Assessment step that checks for DDoS indicators, authentication failures, data exposure risks, and suspicious access patterns.

faq.question: Can junior engineers use this skill effectively?
faq.answer: Yes. The structured workflow guides less experienced team members through incident response phases with clear prompts and expected outputs for each step.

faq.question: What makes this skill different from general debugging guides?
faq.answer: This skill provides a comprehensive incident command system (ICS) with multi-agent orchestration, focusing on coordination, communication, and prevention rather than just technical troubleshooting.
